novel_features.name: BBL document history
novel_features.description: Resolve a borough/block/lot to its full recorded-document history in one call.
novel_features.rationale: ACRIS exposes the BBL-to-document map (Legals) and document details (Master) as separate datasets with no joined endpoint; this command chains them locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent has an address or BBL and needs the chain of title and recordings, this is the single command that returns it.

novel_features.name: BBL debt & mortgage history
novel_features.description: List the mortgage and debt-instrument recordings for a BBL with amounts and dates.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining Legals to Master and filtering to the MORTGAGES & INSTRUMENTS document class — the API has no debt-by-property endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces apparent debt attached to a property without manually cross-referencing three datasets.

novel_features.name: Party substring search
novel_features.description: Find recorded documents by partial party name (grantor, grantee, mortgagor, mortgagee).
novel_features.rationale: The Parties dataset only supports exact equality or full-text $q; this builds the proper SoQL upper(name) LIKE filter for reliable substring lookups.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Entity-driven discovery: find every document an LLC or individual is named on without knowing the exact recorded spelling.

novel_features.name: Full document view
novel_features.description: Assemble one document's master record, all its property (BBL) legals, and all its parties into a single object.
novel_features.rationale: A complete document view requires three separate ACRIS datasets keyed by document_id; this command fans out and merges them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Gives an agent the complete picture of a single recording in one call instead of three.

narrative.headline: Query NYC property records — deeds, mortgages, parties, and full BBL history — straight from NYC Open Data, with cross-dataset joins no raw ACRIS query can do.
narrative.value_prop: ACRIS publishes its property records as four separate NYC Open Data datasets (Master, Legals, Parties, Document Control Codes). This CLI gives you direct, scriptable access to each, plus joined commands — `bbl`, `debt`, `document`, `party-search` — that chain the datasets the way a title search actually needs.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the CLI is wired and NYC Open Data is reachable (no credential required).
See the mortgage document type codes you can filter on.
Resolve a BBL to its recorded documents.
Get the full recorded-document history for that BBL in one call.

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 403 or throttling errors on rapid queries
troubleshoots.fix: Set an app token: export ACRIS_APP_TOKEN=<token from data.cityofnewyork.us>. Anonymous access is rate-limited.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results for a known property
troubleshoots.fix: Block and lot are not zero-padded in ACRIS (use 852 and 134, not 00852/0134); confirm the borough code (1=Manhattan ... 5=Staten Island).

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs NYC real-property record data: who owns or recorded against a property, when it last traded, what mortgages or debt instruments are recorded, or which documents name a given party. It is the right tool for title-research, due-diligence, and portfolio-monitoring workflows over NYC deeds and mortgages.
anti_triggers: Do not use for property valuations, assessments, or tax bills — that is NYC DOF assessment data, not ACRIS.
Do not use for zoning, permits, or violations — those are separate NYC datasets (Zola, DOB).
Do not use for property records outside the five NYC boroughs — ACRIS is NYC-only.
Do not treat results as legal title opinion — ACRIS is the recording index, not a guarantee of title.

recipes.title: Mortgage history for a property
recipes.explanation: Lists mortgage and debt-instrument recordings for the BBL with amounts and dates.

recipes.title: Find documents naming a party
recipes.explanation: Substring search over recorded party names; returns the documents they appear on.

recipes.title: Recent high-value deeds in Manhattan
recipes.explanation: Combines a server-side SoQL filter with --select to keep the payload small for agents.

recipes.title: Full picture of one recording
recipes.explanation: Merges the master record, all property legals (BBLs), and all parties for a single document.

trigger_phrases: find deeds recorded by this LLC
